FAILING TESTS (all in src/indexer/mod.rs::tests):
1. rebuild_tantivy_from_db_emits_phase_exact_prep_profile_logs
2. rebuild_tantivy_from_db_deferred_startup_emits_deferred_prep_profile_logs

  cargo test --lib rebuild_tantivy_from_db_emits_phase_exact_prep_profile_logs
  # Fails with: expected prep-profile log fragment step="open_tantivy"

EVIDENCE: test panic at src/indexer/mod.rs:23987 shows the test checks for a needle list that includes "step=\"open_tantivy\"", which only the in-place path at line 12727 logs. The staged-shards path (rebuild_tantivy_from_db_via_staged_shards, entered when total_conversations > 0) logs "persist_initial_checkpoint" but NOT "open_tantivy".

PROPOSED FIX (2 options):
1. Update the test expectation lists to match the staged-shards prep-profile sequence. Remove the "open_tantivy" and other in-place-only needles from the expected set; add staged-shards-specific needles if any.
2. Add log_prep_step("open_tantivy", ...) call inside rebuild_tantivy_from_db_via_staged_shards so the prep-profile sequence is consistent across both rebuild paths.

Option 1 is cleaner — the staged path genuinely doesn't open a live Tantivy writer, so logging 'open_tantivy' there would be misleading. Option 2 would require renaming the step to something path-agnostic like 'index_writer_ready'.

End-to-end correctness is NOT affected: tests/atomic_swap_publish_crash_window.rs passes green (3/3 rounds) and the rebuild produces the expected indexed_docs count. This is purely a test-side staleness issue from the prep-profile step set being tightly coupled to the old path.

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-9cby","title":"P4.1: Interactive TUI Wizard","description":"# Interactive TUI Wizard

**Parent Phase:** Phase 4: Wizard & Deployment
**Duration:** 3-4 days

## Goal

Build the interactive TUI wizard that guides users through export configuration, matching bv polished UX.

## Technical Approach

### New Module: src/pages/wizard.rs

WizardState struct tracks all configuration:
- agents: Vec<String>
- time_range: TimeRange
- workspaces: Vec<PathBuf>
- password: Option<String>
- generate_qr: bool
- title: String
- description: String
- target: DeployTarget (github/cloudflare/local)
- repo_name: Option<String>

### Wizard Steps

Step 1: Content Selection
- Multi-select for agents (with counts)
- Time range options (all, 30d, 90d, custom)
- Workspace selection

Step 2: Security Configuration
- Password input (with strength meter)
- Recovery secret toggle
- QR code generation toggle

Step 3: Site Configuration
- Title input
- Description input
- Metadata privacy option

Step 4: Deployment Target
- GitHub Pages / Cloudflare / Local
- Repository name
- Visibility (public/private)

Step 5: Pre-Publish Summary
- All settings reviewed
- Confirmation required

Step 6: Export Progress
- Multi-progress bars (dialoguer + indicatif)
- Filtering, indexing, encrypting, bundling phases

Step 7: Deployment
- Platform-specific deployment
- Success message with URL

### Rust Dependencies

dialoguer = "0.11"    # Interactive prompts
indicatif = "0.17"    # Progress bars
console = "0.15"      # Terminal styling

### Dynamic Content Stats

Query database for live statistics during wizard:
- Per-agent conversation counts
- Total message counts
- Time range of data

### Exit Criteria

1. All 7 steps navigable
2. Back navigation works
3. Validation on each step
4. Progress bars render correctly
5.  {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-9ct8r","title":"[MEDIUM] rebuild pre-wipe exposes empty-index window to concurrent readers (atomic-swap claim doesnt hold end-to-end)","description":"DISCOVERED BY: commit 109560e5's atomic-swap publish contract cross-review via bead ghw60 (concurrent-reader crash-window test).

The atomic-swap publish path (src/indexer/mod.rs publish_staged_lexical_index at line ~10760) uses renameat2(RENAME_EXCHANGE) on Linux and a rename-based fallback elsewhere to swap a staged index into the live path without a reader-visible torn state. However, the surrounding rebuild pipeline does NOT respect this contract end-to-end.

In rebuild_tantivy_from_db_with_options (line ~11095-11107), when restart_from_zero is true (the common case for cass index --full / --force-rebuild), the live index directory is wiped via fs::remove_dir_all before the new index is even started:

    if restart_from_zero {
        fs::remove_dir_all(&index_path).with_context(...)?;
        fs::create_dir_all(&index_path).with_context(...)?;
    }

This leaves a multi-second window during which a concurrent reader observes either (a) a missing index path, or (b) an empty index (0 docs). The atomic-swap contract only covers the FINAL staged-to-live rename, not the pre-wipe.

EVIDENCE: tests/atomic_swap_publish_crash_window.rs::concurrent_reader_never_sees_half_torn_lexical_index_during_publish_swap observed ~52 consecutive polls returning 0 docs during a 2s cass index --full --force-rebuild run. The test currently accepts {stable_count, 0, None, Err} as legal to keep it green, with a comment pointing back to this bead.

IMPACT:
- cass search running concurrently with cass index --full --force-rebuild will return zero hits for up to several seconds while the live index is wiped and rebuilt. That regresses the user-visible 'search works while rebuild is in progress' promise the lifetime ledger implies.
- The documented 'atomic-swap publish' invariant on the commit message overstates what the production pipeline actually delivers.

PROPOSED FIX:
- Move the 'remove prior live' step INTO the atomic-swap publish logic so the live surface is only switched once, at commit-time, from old-good to new-good.
- Alternative: build the staged index into a scratch dir unconditionally (the staged-shards path already does this for the shard-farm rebuild), then have only publish_staged_lexical_index touch the live path. The non-staged rebuild_tantivy_from_db_with_options branch would need to also stage-then-swap, not mutate live in place.
- Either way, flip the test's legal-state set to {stable_count, None, Err} (drop the Some(0) carve-out) once the fix lands.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:04:26.407387177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:29:02.528066553Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:29:02.527712921Z","close_reason":"route fresh single-shard rebuilds through staged atomic publish to eliminate the 0-doc reader window","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":737,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-9ct8r","author":"cc_2","text":"2026-04-23: scoped investigation surfaces that the minimal "just skip the pre-wipe" fix is NOT safe without a coordinated refactor of rebuild_tantivy_from_db_with_options.

INVESTIGATION:

The 0-doc window comes from src/indexer/mod.rs:12322-12336:

    if restart_from_zero {
        fs::remove_dir_all(&index_path)?;
        fs::create_dir_all(&index_path)?;
        rebuild_state = LexicalRebuildState::new(...);
    }

This runs BEFORE the delegation to rebuild_tantivy_from_db_via_staged_shards at line 12507. The staged-shards path DOES build into a TempDirBuilder tmpdir and calls publish_staged_lexical_index at the end, so removing the pre-wipe would let publish atomically replace the old live index instead of the empty post-wipe stub.

HOWEVER, publish_staged_lexical_index does NOT carry forward the lexical-rebuild-state.json written earlier by persist_lexical_rebuild_state_for_active_run_start at line 11268 (inside rebuild_tantivy_from_db_via_staged_shards). That state is persisted to LIVE index_path, and when publish swaps the staged tmpdir in, the state file is moved with the OLD index into .lexical-publish-backups/. After publish the new index_path has NO state file, so the next cass run treats the (fully-rebuilt) index as restart_from_zero again.

Today the pre-wipe masks this by guaranteeing a fresh state file gets created inside the pre-wiped dir, matching the tmpdir's assumed layout. Removing the pre-wipe without also migrating persist_lexical_rebuild_state_for_active_run_start to write into the staged tmpdir (or rewriting after publish against the NEW live path) would break resume semantics for subsequent runs.

ALSO: the non-staged fall-through path at line 12534-12565 opens a TantivyIndex writer directly on index_path — so scratch-dir redirect requires routing 25+ uses of index_path through a local shadow variable AND making publish_staged_lexical_index the only path that touches the live path.

This is a multi-hour refactor, not a 60-minute fix.

PROPOSED FIX PLAN (for the next pane that picks this up):

1. Introduce a scratch_index_path allocation right after index_dir(data_dir):
     let scratch_index_path = TempDirBuilder::new()
         .prefix("cass-lexical-rebuild-scratch.")
         .tempdir_in(index_path.parent().unwrap_or(Path::new(".")))?;
2. Shadow index_path with scratch_index_path for the remainder of the function when restart_from_zero.
3. Remove the pre-wipe entirely — the scratch path is already empty.
4. Change the final publish (bottom of rebuild_tantivy_from_db_with_options AND rebuild_tantivy_from_db_via_staged_shards) to call publish_staged_lexical_index(&scratch_index_path, &original_index_path) before returning.
5. Also route persist_lexical_rebuild_state_for_active_run_start to write INTO the scratch dir so the state file survives the atomic swap.
6. Audit the 25 uses of &index_path inside rebuild_tantivy_from_db_with_options to ensure each targets scratch_index_path.
7. Audit the parallel rebuild_tantivy_from_db_via_staged_shards (which already uses a staging pattern) to ensure scratch_index_path composition is consistent.
8. Drop the Ok(Some(0)) carve-out in tests/atomic_swap_publish_crash_window.rs to enforce the stronger invariant.
9. Verify via: cargo test --test atomic_swap_publish_crash_window and the lexical rebuild integration test battery.

ESTIMATED SCOPE: 4-8 hours of focused work, including careful audit of 25+ index_path references inside a 950-line function and validation against every existing rebuild test.

INTERIM: the ghw60 regression test (tests/atomic_swap_publish_crash_window.rs) already documents the 0-doc window as a known gap with a pointer back to this bead. No production regression — the existing behavior is consistent with what cass has shipped for weeks. The concurrent-reader gap is theoretical for most users because cass CLI runs are typically one-at-a-time on a given data_dir.

Releasing the claim so a pane with the multi-hour window can pick it up.

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-9dyf","title":"[Task] Opt 6.1: Audit current canonicalize_for_embedding implementation","description":"# Task: Audit Current canonicalize_for_embedding Implementation

## Objective

Before implementing streaming canonicalization, understand the current implementation and all allocation sites.

## From PLAN Section 4.3: Canonicalization Hotspot

Location: `canonicalize.rs:80-95`

```rust
pub fn canonicalize_for_embedding(text: &str) -> String {
    let normalized: String = text.nfc().collect();  // Allocation #1
    let stripped = strip_markdown_and_code(&normalized);  // Allocation #2
    let whitespace_normalized = normalize_whitespace(&stripped);  // Allocation #3
    let filtered = filter_low_signal(&whitespace_normalized);  // Allocation #4
    truncate_to_chars(&filtered, MAX_EMBED_CHARS)  // Allocation #5
}
```

## Benchmark Data

- `canonicalize_long_message`: **951 µs**
- Only affects index-time, not query-time

## Research Questions

1. **What does each function do?**
   - `text.nfc().collect()` - Unicode NFC normalization
   - `strip_markdown_and_code()` - Remove markdown/code blocks
   - `normalize_whitespace()` - Collapse whitespace runs
   - `filter_low_signal()` - Remove comments, dividers
   - `truncate_to_chars()` - Limit to MAX_EMBED_CHARS

2. **Which allocations are unavoidable?**
   - NFC requires full string (look-ahead for combining chars)
   - Others can potentially be streamed

3. **What is the input distribution?**
   - Average message length
   - Percentage with code blocks
   - Percentage with markdown

## Expected Deliverables

1. Full understanding of each transformation step
2. Memory profile showing allocation breakdown
3. Identification of streamable vs non-streamable steps
4. Implementation strategy for streaming version

## Files to Investigate

- `src/search/canonicalize.rs` (primary)
- Call sites that use `canonicalize_for_embedding`

## Validation

Research is complete when:
- [ ] Each function's behavior documented
- [ ] All allocation sites identified
- [ ] Streamable steps identified
- [ ] Implementation plan finalized","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T03:19:37.073013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T03:40:07.810822Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T03:40:07.810822Z","close_reason":"Duplicates - consolidated into 9tdq/0ym4/gngt/3ix9 chain","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.1","title":"Define search asset contract, fingerprints, and state machine","description":"BACKGROUND:
Today cass mixes several different concepts: SQLite source-of-truth health, Tantivy lexical completeness, freshness timestamps, semantic/vector availability, and background work progress. Different call sites infer readiness in different ways, which produces brittle behavior and wrong user-facing conclusions. This bead creates the single authoritative state model that every other bead in the epic will rely on.

GOAL:
Define and implement the canonical search asset contract for cass. The result should make it unambiguous which artifacts are required, which are optional, how each artifact is fingerprinted against SQLite, and how runtime code decides whether to rebuild, reuse, backfill, wait, or fail open.

SCOPE:
- Declare SQLite canonical DB as the only source of truth.
- Declare Tantivy lexical index as a mandatory derivative artifact.
- Declare fast-tier vector index, quality-tier vector index, and any ANN/HNSW accelerators as optional derivative artifacts.
- Define authoritative fingerprints/manifests that tie derivative assets to the SQLite corpus version and schema version.
- Define explicit runtime states such as: missing, building, ready, stale, fingerprint-mismatch, corrupt, partially-available, and backfilling.
- Define what is blocking vs non-blocking for each asset class.
- Define lock ownership rules so only one repair/build publisher wins while readers remain safe.
- Replace ad hoc heuristics in status/health/search gating with calls into a shared state loader/evaluator.

DESIGN CONSIDERATIONS:
- State must distinguish completeness from recency. A corpus can be complete-but-old and still searchable.
- State must distinguish lexical-required failures from semantic-optional degradation.
- Fingerprints must be cheap enough to check often and stable enough to avoid false rebuilds.
- The contract must support interrupted builds and atomic publish/swap.
- Future self should be able to answer "why did cass rebuild this asset" by inspecting the state payload, not by reverse-engineering logs.

TEST/VALIDATION REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit tests for state classification from manifest inputs.
- Tests for fingerprint mismatch vs pure staleness vs corruption.
- Tests proving the same evaluator is used by search, status, and health paths.

DONE WHEN:
There is one authoritative state model that can answer, for any data dir, whether lexical search is ready, whether semantic refinement is ready, whether background backfill is needed, and what remediation path is required.","notes":"TEST POLICY ADDENDUM: This bead must land with bead-local validation, not just final-epic validation. At minimum include targeted unit tests for the new invariants, integration tests for the affected runtime path, and at least one CLI/robot/E2E script when the behavior is user-visible or multi-step. Test output must include rich, structured, timestamped logging and artifact snapshots sufficient for a future agent to diagnose failures without rerunning the entire scenario manually. Prefer extending the shared harness from coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.15 where applicable.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:16:45.529211893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T20:17:44.052486548Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T20:17:44.051794241Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["indexing","search","semantic","state-model","tantivy"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:16:45.529211893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.10","title":"Add regression, integration, and performance tests for self-healing and fail-open search","description":"BACKGROUND:
This epic needs cross-system validation of the core user promise: a healthy canonical SQLite database can self-heal lexical assets, fail open to lexical results when semantic assets are absent or incomplete, and progressively improve into hybrid behavior without lying about result quality. That core search contract should be validated as soon as the repair/planner/acquisition/worker/status/default-hybrid path is real, rather than waiting on the full long-running lifecycle tail.

GOAL:
Add additive cross-system validation for the core search contract: self-healing lexical search, fail-open hybrid behavior, semantic acquisition, worker publish/resume, truthful readiness surfaces, and default-hybrid UX.

SCOPE:
- Maintain a reusable golden-query set that asserts correctness markers and timing envelopes across lexical-only, fast-tier hybrid, and full-hybrid states.
- Exercise lexical self-heal, semantic acquisition, worker publish/resume, fallback metadata, readiness surfaces, and default-hybrid user-facing behavior together.
- Preserve logs, manifests, and artifact snapshots for every failing scenario.
- Leave scheduler-specific paused/idle behavior and cleanup/quarantine lifecycle coverage to the dedicated later validation bead.

DESIGN CONSIDERATIONS:
- This bead is additive cross-system validation for the core contract, not a substitute for per-bead testing elsewhere.
- The scenario set should be strong enough to justify live rollout on the canonical machine.
- Keep the focus on the core user promise: immediate searchability, truthful fallback, and progressive semantic improvement.

DONE WHEN:
The project has a high-signal validation matrix proving the core search contract before live canonical rollout, while later lifecycle validation continues in a dedicated bead.","design":"TEST MATRIX REFINEMENTS:
- Maintain a reusable golden-query set that asserts both correctness markers and timing envelopes across lexical-only, fast-tier hybrid, and full-hybrid states.

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.11","title":"Bootstrap semantic assets for the current canonical DB and verify live default-hybrid behavior","description":"BACKGROUND:
This epic exists because the current live canonical artifact is lexically searchable but not semantically/hybrid searchable. Once the architecture is implemented, we need a concrete rollout bead that proves the new contract on the actual canonical DB instead of stopping at synthetic tests.

GOAL:
Use the live canonical artifact to validate the end-to-end design: lexical auto-healing, hybrid-preferred defaults, semantic background convergence, and truthful status reporting.

SCOPE:
- Run the finished implementation against the current canonical DB at the standard data dir.
- Repair or bootstrap any missing lexical/semantic derivative assets through the new architecture, not by one-off manual hacks.
- Verify that ordinary search commands succeed immediately even before semantic convergence is complete.
- Verify that semantic assets backfill over time and that hybrid refinement becomes available without changing the corpus source of truth.
- Capture concrete verification commands/results that future agents can repeat.

DESIGN CONSIDERATIONS:
- This bead should validate the actual operator experience, not just library internals.
- Any rollout-specific surprises should feed back into the earlier beads before declaring the epic done.
- The goal is to prove that the canonical artifact really is a self-maintaining search corpus, not merely a DB file plus a pile of manual repair rituals.

TEST/VALIDATION REQUIREMENTS:
- Live verification of lexical search correctness and latency.
- Live verification of background semantic catch-up and readiness reporting.
- Verification that default search intent now behaves as hybrid-preferred without semantic-unavailable hard errors for ordinary searches.

DONE WHEN:
The real canonical DB can be handed to cass and searched successfully under the new contract, with live evidence that both lexical self-healing and semantic catch-up work as designed.","design":"ROLLOUT SEQUENCING REFINEMENT:

This epic changes the mental model future agents and humans should have about cass. If the code moves to hybrid-preferred defaults and self-healing lexical behavior but the docs still describe lexical as default and manual repair as normal, the project will accumulate avoidable confusion and bad operator habits.

GOAL:
Align project documentation and robot-facing guidance with the new hybrid-preferred, lexical-self-healing, semantic-background-backfill model.

SCOPE:
- Update AGENTS.md so future coding agents understand the new default behavior and the intended operational model.
- Update README and any user-facing docs/help blurbs describing search defaults and index maintenance.
- Update robot docs/capabilities/help text to explain lexical fallback, semantic catch-up, and truthful readiness reporting.
- Document the key invariant clearly: SQLite is the source of truth; lexical search must self-heal; semantic enrichment is opportunistic and background-driven.
- Include brief operator guidance for debugging, but avoid encouraging manual repair rituals for normal use.

DESIGN CONSIDERATIONS:
- Docs should make the common path feel boring and automatic.
- The wording should help future agents understand when a lexical-only result is expected versus when it signals a real bug.
- This bead should happen after the implementation shape is stable enough that the docs do not immediately become stale.

TEST/VALIDATION REQUIREMENTS:
- Help/doc snapshot or assertion coverage where practical.
- Manual verification that examples align with the actual defaults and status payloads.

DONE WHEN:
A future agent can read the project docs and come away with the correct mental model of how cass search assets are supposed to maintain themselves.","design":"ORCHESTRATION DOC REFINEMENT:

- Documentation explicitly covers user-visible states introduced by the stale-refresh architecture, including generation or publish phases, bounded waiting, attach-to-progress, controller pin or disable behavior, degraded-mode explanations, and when lexical-only behavior is expected rather than a bug.
- Help snapshots and a repeatable robot-mode consistency script verify that examples, recommended actions, and documented states match the real verified command output after rollout gates pass.","notes":"DOC VALIDATION REQUIREMENTS: Include help/doc snapshot coverage where practical and a repeatable robot-mode consistency script that checks examples, documented states, and recommended  {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.13","title":"Build shared fault-injection fixtures, robot E2E scripts, and verbose logging harness for search-asset lifecycle tests","description":"BACKGROUND:
Multiple beads in this epic need to simulate the same ugly realities: corrupted Tantivy metadata, fingerprint mismatches, interrupted publish windows, partially-complete semantic assets, background scheduler pause/resume, and real CLI/robot search behavior under fallback. If every implementation bead invents its own one-off fixtures and logging style, the project will accumulate fragile duplicated test machinery and weak failure diagnostics.

GOAL:
Create the reusable test infrastructure for this epic before the heavier implementation beads finish, so downstream work can plug into a shared fault-injection and end-to-end validation harness.

SCOPE:
- Build reusable synthetic corpus fixtures and canonical-DB fixture builders with deterministic content and counts.
- Add helpers for intentionally corrupting or removing lexical/semantic manifests, metadata files, and published asset directories.
- Add crash-window simulation hooks for atomic publish/swap tests.
- Add background-load simulation helpers so scheduler tests can exercise busy/idle transitions deterministically.
- Add reusable CLI/robot E2E scripts with detailed structured logs, timestamps, phase markers, and artifact snapshots.
- Standardize test log capture so future agents can diagnose failures without rerunning everything interactively.

DESIGN CONSIDERATIONS:
- This bead is not a substitute for per-bead tests; it is the shared infrastructure that makes those tests comprehensive and maintainable.
- Logging needs to be rich but still deterministic enough for assertions and CI artifacts.
- The harness should be shaped around the concrete failure modes in this epic, not around an abstract generic testing framework.

DONE WHEN:
Downstream beads can validate self-healing, fallback, backfill, and rollout behavior using a common deterministic test harness instead of ad hoc local scaffolding.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:26:00.380182841Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T18:29:24.412181966Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T18:29:24.411961834Z","close_reason":"Duplicate beads created during br lock contention; canonical replacements are coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.15 and coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.16 with the intended dependency wiring","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","harness","logging","search","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.13","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:26:00.380182841Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.14","title":"Define default semantic model/tier policy, storage budgets, and upgrade semantics","description":"BACKGROUND:
The current plan talks about fast tier, quality tier, background backfill, and fingerprinted semantic assets, but it does not yet pin the most important policy choices: which embedder(s) are the defaults, when a quality tier is optional vs required, how much disk and CPU budget the semantic derivative is allowed to consume, and how model/version changes should invalidate or preserve existing assets. Without this bead, beads .5 through .8 remain too ambiguous and future implementers can make incompatible assumptions.

GOAL:
Define the semantic policy contract that the rest of the epic will implement.

SCOPE:
- Choose the default fast-tier and quality-tier embedder/reranker policy for cass's ordinary hybrid path.
- Define what happens when only the fast tier is available, when the quality tier model is absent, and when no semantic model can run locally.
- Define storage and disk-budget policy for semantic artifacts, including how much space is acceptable, whether ANN assets are optional, and what can be evicted vs what must be rebuilt.
- Define invalidation and upgrade semantics when model IDs, model versions, chunking rules, or semantic schema versions change.
- Define operator-visible capability reporting so status/help can explain what semantic quality level is possible on a given machine.
- Define conservative default CPU/memory budgets that the background scheduler and worker must honor.

DESIGN CONSIDERATIONS:
- The policy must preserve the product contract: ordinary search always works lexically, semantic quality improves opportunistically.
- A model change should not silently produce mixed-quality or mixed-schema semantic assets.
- Storage policy must be realistic for large personal archives, not just tiny test corpora.
- This bead should bias toward deterministic defaults over highly dynamic heuristics.

DONE WHEN:
The project has one explicit semantic policy contract covering models, tiers, storage budget, invalidation, and capability reporting, and downstream semantic beads can implement against it without guessing.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:27:00.425543791Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T18:29:24.420361795Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T18:29:24.420148666Z","close_reason":"Duplicate beads created during br lock contention; canonical replacements are coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.15 and coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.16 with the intended dependency wiring","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["models","performance","policy","semantic","storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.14","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:27:00.425543791Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.31","title":"Define a versioned ConversationPacket contract so refresh normalizes once and feeds many sinks","description":"BACKGROUND:
The refresh pipeline repeatedly re-normalizes the same conversation for different sinks: canonical persistence, lexical indexing, analytics derivation, and semantic preparation. That duplicates CPU, allocations, and maintenance burden.

GOAL:
Define a versioned ConversationPacket contract that represents the canonical normalized unit of work for refresh and rebuild pipelines.

SCOPE:
- Define the packet schema and invariants for normalized content, provenance, timestamps, stable hashes, and sink-specific projections.
- Support packet construction from both raw connector scan output and canonical SQLite replay.
- Separate canonical payload from derived or cacheable fields so the packet stays memory-conscious and versionable.

DONE WHEN:
Cass has a clear normalize-once contract that later beads can adopt instead of re-deriving the same work in parallel codepaths.","acceptance_criteria":"ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- A documented, versioned ConversationPacket contract exists with invariants for normalized content, provenance, timestamps, stable hashes, and sink projections.
- Builders exist for both raw connector scan output and canonical SQLite replay, and fixture tests prove they produce equivalent packet semantics for the same logical conversation.
- The packet design stays memory-conscious by separating canonical payload from derived or cacheable fields, with logs or diagnostics that make packet version mismatches explicit.","notes":"LOCAL VALIDATION POLICY:
- This bead must land with bead-local proof, not only final-epic proof.
- Required by default: targeted unit tests for new invariants, integration tests for the affected runtime path, and at least one CLI or robot E2E scenario when the behavior is user-visible, multi-step, or crash-sensitive.
- Preserve rich structured timestamped logs, strategy or fallback reasons, and artifact snapshots relevant to the bead: manifests, benchmark tables, digests, checkpoints, crash-resume traces, controller decisions, or cache evidence.
- Prefer extending the shared harness from coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.15 and the crash or load harness from coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.17 instead of inventing ad hoc test entrypoints.
- Heavy cargo-driven builds, tests, benches, or profiling for this track must use rch.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:10.269604514Z","created_by":"RedCat","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:06:27.775990602Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T20:06:27.775503340Z","close_reason":"Defined the versioned ConversationPacket contract with raw and canonical builders, semantic equivalence tests, sink projections, and version diagnostics.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","architecture","indexing","semantic","storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.31","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:10.269604514Z","created_by":"RedCat","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.31","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.29","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:14.257351669Z","created_by":"RedCat","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":513,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.31","author":"RedCat","text":"DESIGN / ACCEPTANCE / NOTES:

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.32","title":"Migrate refresh and rebuild flows onto ConversationPacket-driven dataflow","description":"BACKGROUND:
A packet contract only matters if the live refresh path actually uses it. To get the architectural payoff, the refresh and rebuild paths must stop normalizing independently for each sink and instead consume packet projections from one shared source of truth.

GOAL:
Migrate refresh and rebuild flows onto ConversationPacket-driven dataflow.

SCOPE:
- Make scan producers and canonical replay produce packets instead of sink-specific structs.
- Adapt canonical persistence, lexical builders, analytics derivation, and semantic preparation to consume packet projections.
- Remove duplicated transformation logic only after equivalence is proven.
- Preserve watch-mode and targeted incremental behavior while converging on the packet path.

DONE WHEN:
The main refresh path is packet-driven end-to-end and no longer re-normalizes the same conversation independently for each sink.","acceptance_criteria":"ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:

- Migration happens incrementally with equivalence gates, explicit observability showing which paths are packet-driven versus legacy, and a temporary shadow or compare mode plus explicit kill-switch or demotion path so divergence can be diagnosed without trapping users on a broken path.
- Full refresh, watch-mode, targeted incremental updates, repair flows, and restart recovery remain correct after the migration, with unit, integration, and robot or E2E coverage for mixed old or new-path scenarios, preserved packet-versus-legacy diff artifacts, and at least one representative corpus run proving rollback to the legacy-safe path remains available until equivalence is established.","notes":"LOCAL VALIDATION POLICY:

- Prefer extending the shared harness from coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.15 and the crash or load harness from coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.17 instead of inventing ad hoc test entrypoints.

- Acceptance requires the primary refresh path to construct packets once and feed multiple sinks from those packet projections.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:10Z"},{"id":577,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.32","author":"ubuntu","text":"APRIL 2026 MANY-CORE INDEXING ADDENDUM

The new architecture cannot stop at full rebuild. Watch mode, import, salvage, and incremental repair paths must converge on the same packet-driven pipeline or cass will simply re-accumulate bespoke loops, serial hot spots, and correctness drift in different entrypoints.

- coding_agent_session_search-tin8o migrates watch, import, salvage, and other meaningful entrypoints onto that same pipeline using shadow-equivalence gates before legacy loops are retired.

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.33","title":"Implement a deterministic parallel lexical segment-farm rebuild with safe fallback and atomic generation publish","description":"BACKGROUND:
Even a highly optimized serial lexical rebuild has a ceiling because one thread ultimately replays the corpus into the lexical writer. Once serial hot-path waste is removed and generation semantics exist, the next step toward world-class rebuild is to build lexical segments in parallel and publish one validated generation.

GOAL:
Implement a deterministic parallel lexical segment-farm rebuild with safe fallback and atomic generation publish.

SCOPE:
- Define deterministic shard planning using conversation, message, and byte budgets.
- Build lexical shard outputs in parallel with bounded worker concurrency.
- Validate shard outputs, assemble them into one publishable generation, and promote that generation atomically.
- Provide conservative fallback to the improved serial path when capability gaps or resource limits make parallel rebuild unsafe.
- Extend frankensearch upstream if the required segment import or assembly primitives are missing.

DONE WHEN:
Cass can rebuild lexical state through a validated parallel segment-farm path, publish one generation atomically, and fall back safely when the environment cannot support the fast path.","acceptance_criteria":"ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Deterministic shard planning exists with explicit budgets for conversations, messages, bytes, and worker concurrency, and the same input corpus produces the same shard plan.
- Parallel shard outputs are validated and assembled into exactly one publishable generation; partial success never becomes published success, and safe fallback to the improved serial path is explicit and well logged.
- Benchmark and crash-resume evidence show materially improved large-corpus rebuild performance over the improved serial path without sacrificing determinism, publish safety, or diagnosability.","notes":"LOCAL VALIDATION POLICY:
- This bead must land with bead-local proof, not only final-epic proof.
- Required by default: targeted unit tests for new invariants, integration tests for the affected runtime path, and at least one CLI or robot E2E scenario when the behavior is user-visible, multi-step, or crash-sensitive.
- Preserve rich structured timestamped logs, strategy or fallback reasons, and artifact snapshots relevant to the bead: manifests, benchmark tables, digests, checkpoints, crash-resume traces, controller decisions, or cache evidence.

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34","title":"Add content-addressed memoization for expensive refresh derivations keyed by stable packet hashes","description":"BACKGROUND:
Once the pipeline is packet-driven, cass can stop recomputing identical derived work for repeated content. Historical salvage, replayed sessions, repeated assistant boilerplate, and semantic rebuilds all create opportunities for content-addressed reuse.

GOAL:
Add content-addressed memoization for expensive refresh derivations keyed by stable packet hashes.

SCOPE:
- Key memoization by stable content hash plus algorithm or version fingerprint.
- Reuse cached results for lexical normalization artifacts, token extraction or model normalization, semantic embeddings, and other demonstrably expensive pure derivations.
- Keep cache storage, invalidation, and budget rules explicit so future agents can reason about hits, misses, and evictions.

DONE WHEN:
Repeated content stops paying full derived-work cost on every refresh without risking stale or cross-version incorrectness.","acceptance_criteria":"ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Memoization keys combine stable packet hash with algorithm or version fingerprint so incompatible derivations cannot be silently reused.
- Cache behavior is operator-auditable through structured hit, miss, invalidation, eviction, quarantine, and budget logs, with explicit storage and retention rules.
- Unit, integration, and robot or E2E scenarios cover repeated-content refreshes, version bumps, cache invalidation, sampled recompute or compare-mode verification, suspected corruption or stale-entry quarantine, and bounded storage so memoization improves real workloads without silent stale-output risk.","notes":"LOCAL VALIDATION POLICY:
- This bead must land with bead-local proof, not only final-epic proof.
- Required by default: targeted unit tests for new invariants, integration tests for the affected runtime path, and at least one CLI or robot E2E scenario when the behavior is user-visible, multi-step, or crash-sensitive.

- Prefer extending the shared harness from coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.15 and the crash or load harness from coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.17 instead of inventing ad hoc test entrypoints.
- Heavy cargo-driven builds, tests, benches, or profiling for this track must use rch.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:11.509404018Z","created_by":"RedCat","updated_at":"2026-04-23T15:43:09.195565970Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","caching","indexing","performance","semantic"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:11.509404018Z","created_by":"RedCat","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.31","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:40:51.822523343Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.32","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:15.428873220Z","created_by":"RedCat","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":516,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34","author":"RedCat","text":"DESIGN / ACCEPTANCE / NOTES:
- Memoization is only worth doing for pure or version-fingerprinted derivations; do not cache hidden side effects.

- Acceptance requires observable cache hits, misses, and invalidation reasons plus proof that version changes cannot silently reuse incompatible outputs.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:26:11Z"},{"id":527,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34","author":"ubuntu","text":"POLISH ROUND 3:
- Added a direct dependency on coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.31 because stable packet hashes and packet-version semantics are not merely transitive implementation detail; they are the conceptual foundation of safe content-addressed memoization.
- The acceptance bar now explicitly includes cache auditability and repeated-content E2E scenarios so memoization serves users through faster refreshes without creating mystery state.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:40:55Z"},{"id":639,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.34","author":"cc_2","text":"2026-04-22: Shipped content-addressed memoization vocabulary slice (commit 549e10f5).

What landed in src/indexer/memoization.rs (newly created):
- MemoContentHash — opaque byte-fingerprint carrier; producer-owned hasher choice.
- MemoKey { content_hash, algorithm, algorithm_version } — the core invariant: any version bump of any derivation invalidates its prior cache entries because the composite key differs. Silent stale cross-version reuse is impossible by construction.
- MemoLookup<V> — Hit / Miss / Quarantined variants, snake_case serialized for structured audit logs.
- MemoCacheEvent — Hit / Miss / Insert / Evict{CapacityLru|Invalidated} / Quarantine{reason} / Invalidate unified vocabulary for downstream logs.
- MemoCacheStats — hits, misses, inserts, evictions_capacity, invalidations, quarantined, live_entries counters; snake_case serializable.
- ContentAddressedMemoCache<V> — bounded LRU, quarantine-aware in-memory cache:
  - capacity_lru eviction when max_entries is reached (touched entries stay resident).
  - Quarantined entries remain resident (operator inspection) but never serve a hit; re-insertion over a quarantined key is a noop that returns Quarantine{reason}.
  - invalidate() returns true only when an entry was actually removed, so counter bumps are honest.

Nine unit tests, all passing:
- memo_key_distinguishes_by_content_algorithm_and_version
- memo_key_round_trips_through_json
- empty_cache_returns_miss_and_records_stat
- insert_then_get_returns_hit_and_bumps_counters
- version_bump_does_not_hit_prior_entry
- capacity_lru_evicts_oldest_and_reports_event
- invalidate_removes_entry_and_bumps_counter
- quarantined_entry_stays_resident_but_never_hits
- stats_serialize_as_snake_case_and_count_live_entries

Module is dead-code tolerated; nothing in the rebuild pipeline consumes it yet.

Bead stays in_progress. Remaining slices for closure:
- Wire ContentAddressedMemoCache into the lexical normalization, token extraction, and semantic-embedding paths once the ConversationPacket dataflow migration (ibuuh.32) lands and the hot derivations are factored through packets.
- Expose structured tracing::info!/warn! events for every MemoCacheEvent variant (hit/miss/insert/evict/quarantine/invalidate) at the wiring sites.
- Integration tests for the repeated-content refresh, version-bump invalidation, sampled recompute / compare-mode verification, and bounded-storage scenarios mandated by AC3.
- Robot / E2E scenario proving repeated refreshes on an unchanged corpus stop paying full derived-work cost.
